issues.description:
Daily Command Center Digest. Mode=create_issue. Runs every day 07:00 Asia/Kuwait (GMT+3). Produces ONE per-project status briefing comment on the dated issue this autopilot creates, so the CEO + workspace owner can see — at a glance — every project's state and what to push today.

CRITICAL SAFETY RULES:
- Mode is create_issue. Do not create additional issues. Do not change status on any other issue.
- NEVER mention any agent or member in the digest comment. Owner reads the dated issue directly; mentions would loop (Factory Pulse no-mention rule).
- NEVER touch DEV-36 (workspace-operations dialog) — exclude it from every list, count, and section.
- Quiet on green days: if NOTHING actionable changed since yesterday's digest (no new blockers, no stale in_review, no new approval ask, no project lifecycle_phase change, no new app-request), do NOT post any comment. Leave the auto-created issue body empty so it can be auto-cancelled by step (8).
- Use `--output json` on every CLI call. Continue on single-call failure (log to stdout and skip that signal).

STEPS:

(1) Snapshot the workspace.
    - `multica project list --output json` → all projects.
    - `multica issue list --output json` → all issues (paged if needed).
    - For each non-cancelled issue: record id, identifier, title, status, project_id, parent_issue_id, assignee_id, assignee_type, updated_at.
    - Exclude DEV-36 (id e60d5be5-b9dd-48aa-b949-28286ea80f1b) everywhere.
    - Build agent + squad name maps once: `multica agent list --output json` and `multica squad list --output json`. Used for assignee names; never for mention links.

(2) Detect "since yesterday" deltas. Treat `since = (now - 24h)` UTC.
    - new_blockers: issues that flipped to status=blocked in the window (updated_at >= since AND status=blocked).
    - cleared_blockers: issues that left blocked in the window (status != blocked AND prior blocked_reason metadata key present — best-effort).
    - new_approval_asks: any issue whose metadata gained `waiting_on=owner` or `waiting_on=admin` in the window, or whose body/comments contain a fresh "Approval needed" line written in the window (cheap heuristic: latest comment created_at >= since and contains "approval needed" case-insensitive).
    - stale_in_review_48h: any issue status=in_review with updated_at older than 48h.
    - lifecycle_phase changes: any parent issue whose metadata `lifecycle_phase` changed (compare to value from yesterday's pin — see step 7).
    - new_app_requests: any issue created_at >= since in project `01 - App Requests` (f3089a97-c608-4599-913c-f331335e1abe).
    If ALL six lists are empty AND there is no stale in_review and no current blocked anywhere → green day. Skip to step (8) without posting.

(3) Per-project section assembly. For each project (sorted by project title prefix `00 -`, `01 -`, … and then APP projects):
    - Skip the project entirely if it has zero open issues (status in {todo, in_progress, in_review, blocked}) AND no new_app_requests AND no lifecycle change.
    - Goal: 1 sentence from project.description (truncate to ~140 chars at the first sentence boundary).
    - lifecycle_phase: read from the project's "anchor" parent issue (heuristic: the parent issue with the most children in this project; if APP project, the parent whose title starts with "APP — "). Show the value of metadata `lifecycle_phase` or "—" if unset.
    - Active issues: counts by status — `todo=N in_progress=N in_review=N blocked=N` (exclude DEV-36).
    - Blockers + waiting_on: list every issue in this project with status=blocked OR metadata `waiting_on` set. Format: `DEV-NN <title> — waiting_on=<value> | blocked_reason=<value>`. Cap 5 per project; "+N more" if truncated.
    - Next action + owner: the single highest-priority issue in this project that is `todo` or `in_progress` and not blocked. Format: `DEV-NN <title> — owner=<agent-or-squad name>` (plain name, NO mention link).
    - Risk: 1 line — pick the most material risk: stale in_review item, oldest blocker, or "WIP imbalance: <name> has N in_progress" if any agent in this project has >=4 open issues. If nothing material, write "Risk: low — no stale or overloaded items.".
    - Approval needed: yes/no. If yes, quote the exact ask (one sentence from the requesting issue's body/comment). Format: `Approval needed: yes — DEV-NN: "<exact ask>"` or `Approval needed: no`.

(4) Compose the Top-3 block. Score every open issue across the workspace by:
    - urgency: blocked=4, stale_in_review_48h=3, has approval ask=3, deploy_gate=red=4, priority=urgent=4 / high=3 / medium=2 / low=1.
    - momentum: in_progress=2, in_review=1, todo=1, backlog=0.
    - age penalty: +1 per 24h since updated_at, cap +3.
    Final score = urgency + momentum + age_penalty. Pick the top 3 distinct issues (no two from the same parent unless nothing else qualifies).

(5) Render the digest comment using this exact shape (parseable by humans and future audits):

```
# Daily Command Center Digest — <YYYY-MM-DD KW>

**Window:** <since> → <now> (UTC) | **Projects scanned:** N | **Open issues:** N | **Blocked:** N | **Stale in_review (48h):** N | **Approval asks:** N

## Per-Project Status

### <project icon> <project title>
- **Goal:** <one sentence>
- **Phase:** <lifecycle_phase>
- **Active:** todo=<n> in_progress=<n> in_review=<n> blocked=<n>
- **Blockers / waiting_on:** <list or "none">
- **Next action:** <DEV-NN title — owner=name>
- **Risk:** <one line>
- **Approval needed:** <yes/no> [— "<exact ask>"]

### <next project>
… (same shape)

## Top 3 to Push Today

1. **DEV-NN** <title> — assignee=<name> | why: <one phrase>
2. **DEV-NN** <title> — assignee=<name> | why: <one phrase>
3. **DEV-NN** <title> — assignee=<name> | why: <one phrase>

## Deltas Since Yesterday
- New blockers: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- Cleared blockers: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- New approval asks: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- Stale in_review (48h+): <DEV-NN list or "none">
- New app requests: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- Lifecycle phase changes: <project: old→new or "none">
```

Post via: `multica issue comment add <this-issue-id> --content-stdin` (HEREDOC the body). NEVER include any `mention://agent/...` or `mention://member/...` link. Issue references should be plain `DEV-NN` text (no `mention://issue/...` either — keep the digest scannable).

(6) On a green day (step 2 short-circuited), do NOT post any comment. Skip to step (8).

(7) Pin metadata on THIS issue only if a value materially helps tomorrow's run:
    - `last_run_verdict` = "green" or "posted" (overwrite each run).
    - `last_window_end` = <RFC3339-now> (overwrite each run).
    - `lifecycle_phase_snapshot` = JSON map `{project_id: phase}` so step (2) can detect phase changes tomorrow.
    - `top3_count` = number (expect 0 on green days, 3 otherwise).
    Use `multica issue metadata set` once per changed key. Skip any key whose value is identical to what `metadata list` already shows.

(8) Status finalization.
    - If a digest was posted: flip THIS issue to `in_review` (`multica issue status <this-issue-id> in_review`). CEO + owner read it, then close.
    - If green day (no digest posted): flip THIS issue to `cancelled` with no comment so it stops cluttering the inbox.

(9) Output one line to stdout (only non-comment output): `Daily Digest @ <RFC3339> | verdict=<green|posted> | projects=N | top3=N | blocked=B stale=S approvals=A`.

OPERATING RULES:
- Never block on a single CLI failure. Log "skipped <signal>: <reason>" and continue with the remaining signals.
- All comments go ONLY on the issue this autopilot creates. Never comment on any other issue.
- Never mention any agent or member anywhere. Plain names only.
- Read this issue's existing metadata before pinning. Only update keys whose values changed.
- Do not create sub-issues, do not reassign any other issue, do not change any other issue's status.
- The created issue MUST land in `in_review` (when posted) or `cancelled` (green day) — never `done` (owner closes), never left in `todo`.

---
*Autopilot run triggered at 2026-05-30 07:00 Asia/Kuwait. After starting work, rename this issue to accurately reflect what you are doing.*
issues.title: Daily Command Center Digest — 2026-05-30

issues.description:
You are working on an existing project called:

NewTech Command OS

Important:
Do NOT rebuild from scratch.
Do NOT create a new prototype.
Do NOT replace the current app with a generic dashboard.
Continue from the current codebase and upgrade it into a production-ready operations platform.

Current project context:

- Project name: NewTech Command OS
- Business: NewTech Kuwait operations platform
- Domain: electronics retail, car accessories, smart home, installation, delivery, maintenance
- Current stack: React 19, Vite, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS 4, Express
- Current persistence: file-based JSON database
- Current backend file: server.ts is too large and contains most API routes
- Current modules already exist:
   - Dashboard
   - Orders
   - Dispatch Board
   - Technicians
   - Drivers
   - CRM
   - Warehouse
   - Payments
   - WhatsApp
   - Reports
   - AI Command Center
   - Audit Logs
   - Settings
- Current API endpoints already exist for:
   - health
   - bootstrap
   - orders
   - technicians
   - drivers
   - inventory
   - crm tickets
   - settings
   - whatsapp send
   - payment verify
   - mobile jobs
   - Shopify webhook
   - MyFatoorah webhook
   - AI command

Current critical issues to fix:

1. No real database. Current database is database.json.
2. No real authentication or auth middleware.
3. No Zod validation.
4. No rate limiting.
5. server.ts is too large and must be modularized.
6. No pagination. /api/bootstrap returns all data.
7. No webhook retry queue.
8. No structured logging.
9. No unit tests or E2E tests.
10. Some invalid Tailwind classes exist.
11. Package name is still react-example.
12. Mock secrets exist in mock data and must be removed.
13. Phone normalization is inconsistent.
14. No individual mutation loading states.
15. No production deployment setup.

Your mission:
Upgrade the existing NewTech Command OS from a strong prototype into a production-ready system.

Business rules:

- Shopify remains the source of truth for products, variants, prices, discounts, and online checkout.
- NewTech Command OS is the operations brain above Shopify.
- MyFatoorah payments must never be marked paid from frontend only.
- Payment verification must happen server-side.
- WhatsApp and external API secrets must never be exposed in frontend.
- Every admin action must create audit log.
- Every integration action must create integration log.
- Every order status change must create timeline event.
- Every webhook must be idempotent.
- Kuwait phone numbers must normalize to +965XXXXXXXX.
- Arabic RTL must remain first-class.
- Do not remove existing useful features.

Target production stack for this existing project:

- React 19
- Vite
- TypeScript strict mode
- Tailwind CSS 4
- Express modular backend
- PostgreSQL
- Drizzle ORM
- Zod
- JWT auth
- bcrypt
- RBAC middleware
- Redis
- BullMQ
- Pino logger
- express-rate-limit
- Vitest
- Playwright
- Docker
- GitHub Actions

# ==================================================

PHASE 0 — CODEBASE REVIEW

First, inspect the current codebase.

Output:

1. Existing files summary
2. Existing modules summary
3. Current API route summary
4. Current data model summary
5. Critical risks
6. Safe migration strategy
7. Files you will change
8. What you will not touch yet

Rules:

- Do not start by deleting working features.
- Do not do a full rewrite.
- Refactor gradually.
- Keep the app running after every phase.

# ==================================================

PHASE 1 — CLEANUP AND SAFETY FIXES

Do these first:

1. Rename package from react-example to newtech-command-os.
2. Remove fake secrets from mockData.ts.
3. Move all secret-like values to .env.example only.
4. Fix invalid Tailwind classes:
   - bg-zinc-105
   - bg-red-105
   - text-zinc-650
   - text-zinc-850
   - text-zinc-805
5. Add centralized config loader.
6. Add centralized logger using Pino.
7. Add global API error handler.
8. Add React ErrorBoundary.
9. Add loading state and error state for every mutation.
10. Add consistent phone normalization utility:
   - normalizeKuwaitPhone(input) => +965XXXXXXXX
      - reject invalid Kuwait numbers
11. Add audit log helper.
12. Add integration log helper.

Acceptance:

- App still runs.
- No fake secrets remain in frontend/mock data.
- No invalid Tailwind classes remain.
- Errors are handled consistently.
- Logs are structured.

# ==================================================

PHASE 2 — MODULARIZE BACKEND

Refactor server.ts into modular backend.

Create structure:

server/
├── index.ts
├── app.ts
├── config/
│   └── env.ts
├── db/
│   ├── index.ts
│   ├── schema.ts
│   ├── migrate.ts
│   └── seed.ts
├── middleware/
│   ├── auth.ts
│   ├── rbac.ts
│   ├── errorHandler.ts
│   ├── rateLimit.ts
│   └── validate.ts
├── utils/
│   ├── phone.ts
│   ├── audit.ts
│   ├── integrationLog.ts
│   ├── idempotency.ts
│   └── logger.ts
├── modules/
│   ├── auth/
│   ├── orders/
│   ├── technicians/
│   ├── drivers/
│   ├── inventory/
│   ├── crm/
│   ├── payments/
│   ├── whatsapp/
│   ├── shopify/
│   ├── zoho/
│   ├── ai/
│   ├── mobile/
│   ├── reports/
│   ├── settings/
│   └── audit/
└── jobs/
    ├── queues.ts
    ├── workers.ts
    ├── webhookJobs.ts
    └── whatsappJobs.ts

Rules:

- Keep existing API behavior working.
- Preserve endpoint URLs initially.
- Move one module at a time.
- Do not break frontend API client.
- Keep backward compatibility.

Acceptance:

- server.ts is reduced or replaced.
- Routes are separated by module.
- App still runs.
- All existing endpoints still respond.

# ==================================================

PHASE 3 — POSTGRESQL + DRIZZLE ORM

Replace file-based JSON persistence with PostgreSQL + Drizzle ORM.

Add packages:

- drizzle-orm
- postgres
- drizzle-kit

Create database schema for:

- users
- roles
- permissions
- user_roles
- customers
- addresses
- orders
- order_items
- order_services
- order_timeline
- technicians
- drivers
- inventory_items
- inventory_locations
- inventory_movements
- stock_reservations
- crm_tickets
- ticket_messages
- payments
- whatsapp_templates
- whatsapp_messages
- audit_logs
- integration_logs
- settings
- ai_commands
- mobile_sessions

Migration rules:

- Preserve current mock seed data.
- Create seed.ts to load current mock data into PostgreSQL.
- Keep JSON fallback only for development if DATABASE_URL is missing.
- Never use JSON database in production.

Environment variables:

- DATABASE_URL
- NODE_ENV

Acceptance:

- Data persists in PostgreSQL.
- Existing pages load from database-backed API.
- Seed command works.
- No production path depends on database.json.

# ==================================================

PHASE 4 — AUTHENTICATION + RBAC

Add real authentication.

User types:

- super_admin
- operations_manager
- customer_service
- warehouse_keeper
- technician
- driver
- accountant
- marketing

Implement:

1. Login endpoint
2. JWT access token
3. bcrypt password hashing
4. Auth middleware
5. RBAC middleware
6. Current user endpoint
7. Logout endpoint
8. Mobile login for technicians/drivers
9. Route protection by permission
10. Frontend login screen
11. Remove role switcher from production mode
12. Keep dev role switcher only when NODE_ENV=development

Permissions:

- view_dashboard
- view_orders
- create_orders
- update_orders
- assign_orders
- cancel_orders
- view_dispatch
- manage_dispatch
- view_customers
- manage_customers
- view_inventory
- manage_inventory
- view_payments
- verify_payments
- view_whatsapp
- send_whatsapp
- view_reports
- view_settings
- manage_settings
- view_audit_logs

Acceptance:

- Protected routes require login.
- API rejects unauthorized requests.
- UI hides forbidden modules.
- Audit logs include user ID.

# ==================================================

PHASE 5 — ZOD VALIDATION + API CONTRACT

Add Zod validation for all API inputs.

Create schemas for:

- createOrder
- updateOrder
- assignTechnician
- assignDriver
- createTicket
- addTicketMessage
- transferInventory
- sendWhatsApp
- verifyPayment
- updateSettings
- mobileJobUpdate
- webhook payload validation

Rules:

- Reject invalid input with clear error messages.
- Normalize phone before saving.
- Validate enum statuses.
- Validate payment amounts.
- Validate order status transitions.

Acceptance:

- All mutation endpoints use validation middleware.
- Invalid data cannot mutate DB.
- Frontend shows validation errors properly.

# ==================================================

PHASE 6 — ORDER ENGINE HARDENING

Improve order workflow.

Implement:

1. Order state transition service
2. Payment state transition service
3. Timeline event on every order update
4. Audit log on every admin action
5. Stock reservation check
6. Appointment conflict check
7. Technician skill check
8. Driver availability check
9. COD rules
10. Warranty creation flow
11. Support ticket creation from order

Rules:

- Cannot assign delivery unless order is ready_for_dispatch.
- Cannot assign technician if skill mismatch unless manager overrides.
- Cannot complete installation unless technician started work.
- Cannot mark MyFatoorah paid manually without special permission.
- Manual payment override must create audit log with reason.
- Every state change must be idempotent-safe.

Acceptance:

- Order actions are safe.
- Timeline is complete.
- Audit log is complete.
- Business rules are enforced server-side, not only frontend.

# ==================================================

PHASE 7 — PAGINATION + PERFORMANCE

Fix unbounded bootstrap.

Implement:

1. Paginated list endpoints:
   - GET /api/orders?page=&limit=&status=&q=
   - GET /api/customers?page=&limit=&q=
   - GET /api/crm/tickets?page=&limit=&status=
   - GET /api/whatsapp/messages?page=&limit=
   - GET /api/audit-logs?page=&limit=
   - GET /api/integration-logs?page=&limit=
2. Keep /api/bootstrap only for dashboard summary and small reference data.
3. Add database indexes:
   - orders.order_number
   - orders.customer_phone
   - orders.order_status
   - orders.payment_status
   - orders.created_at
   - crm_tickets.status
   - audit_logs.created_at
   - integration_logs.created_at
4. Add frontend pagination controls.
5. Add search debounce.
6. Memoize heavy derived lists.

Acceptance:

- Bootstrap no longer returns all historical data.
- Large order lists perform well.
- Frontend remains responsive.

# ==================================================

PHASE 8 — WEBHOOK RELIABILITY + QUEUES

Add Redis + BullMQ.

Queues:

- webhookQueue
- whatsappQueue
- integrationQueue
- aiQueue
- reportsQueue

Implement:

1. Webhook ingestion endpoints respond fast.
2. Store webhook event with idempotency key.
3. Process webhook in background.
4. Retry failed jobs.
5. Add dead-letter handling.
6. Add queue status page in Settings/Admin.
7. Add integration logs for success/failure.

Shopify:

- orders/create
- orders/paid
- orders/updated
- orders/cancelled
- refunds/create

MyFatoorah:

- payment status webhook
- verify payment job
- signature verification

WhatsApp:

- send template job
- receive status webhook
- retry failed messages

Acceptance:

- Webhooks are idempotent.
- Failed webhooks retry.
- Admin can see integration failures.
- No duplicate orders/payments from repeated webhooks.

# ==================================================

PHASE 9 — REAL INTEGRATION PLACEHOLDERS

Prepare production-ready integration services.

Shopify:

- test connection
- sync products
- sync orders
- webhook signature verification
- map Shopify financial_status to local paymentStatus
- map Shopify fulfillment_status to local orderStatus

MyFatoorah:

- create payment link
- verify payment
- webhook signature verification
- never trust frontend redirect only

WhatsApp:

- WATI provider
- WhatsApp Cloud API provider
- send template
- message status tracking
- failed retry

Zoho:

- sync customer to CRM
- create Books invoice
- record payment
- create Desk ticket
- sync Inventory item

Gemini:

- server-side AI command
- no GEMINI_API_KEY in frontend
- save AI responses
- require approval before AI actions

Acceptance:

- Services are server-side only.
- Secrets read from env only.
- Missing credentials show mock mode.
- Every action writes integration log.

# ==================================================

PHASE 10 — FRONTEND IMPROVEMENTS

Improve UI without changing product direction.

Add:

1. Mutation loading indicators.
2. Toast notifications.
3. Better error messages.
4. Empty states.
5. Confirmation modals.
6. Better filter bars.
7. Pagination.
8. Better RTL spacing.
9. Better mobile responsive layout.
10. ErrorBoundary per main page.
11. Safe optimistic updates only where appropriate.

Keep:

- Existing NewTech premium black/white/gold style.
- Existing Arabic RTL direction.
- Existing pages and workflows.

Acceptance:

- UI feels production-grade.
- Staff can understand what is happening.
- No silent failures.

# ==================================================

PHASE 11 — FILE UPLOADS

Add upload support for:

- Proof of delivery signature
- Proof of delivery photo
- Before installation photos
- After installation photos
- Customer attachments
- Ticket attachments

Use:

- S3-compatible storage if configured
- Local dev storage fallback only in development

Environment variables:

- S3_ENDPOINT
- S3_BUCKET
- S3_ACCESS_KEY_ID
- S3_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY
- S3_PUBLIC_BASE_URL

Acceptance:

- Files are not stored as base64 in DB.
- Attachments are linked to orders/tickets.
- Upload permissions are checked.

# ==================================================

PHASE 12 — TESTING

Add tests.

Unit tests with Vitest:

- phone normalization
- order status transition
- payment status transition
- technician scoring
- driver scoring
- webhook idempotency
- Zod validation
- audit log helper

E2E tests with Playwright:

- login
- view orders
- create order
- assign technician
- assign driver
- verify payment mock
- send WhatsApp mock
- create ticket
- inventory transfer

Acceptance:

- npm test works.
- E2E smoke test works.
- Critical business rules covered.

# ==================================================

PHASE 13 — CI/CD + DEPLOYMENT

Prepare production deployment.

Add:

1. Dockerfile
2. docker-compose.yml for local dev:
   - app
   - postgres
   - redis
3. GitHub Actions:
   - install
   - typecheck
   - lint
   - test
   - build
4. .env.example complete
5. README updated
6. [SECURITY.md](http://SECURITY.md)
7. [DEPLOYMENT.md](http://DEPLOYMENT.md)
8. backup strategy docs

Deployment target:

- Coolify / VPS / Railway / Render

Acceptance:

- Clean build.
- Docker works.
- CI passes.
- No secrets committed.

# ==================================================

PHASE 14 — MOBILE OPERATIONS APP PREP

Prepare API and plan for mobile app.

Mobile roles:

- technician
- driver
- warehouse_keeper
- operations_manager

Required mobile APIs:

- POST /api/mobile/login
- GET /api/mobile/me
- GET /api/mobile/jobs
- PATCH /api/mobile/jobs/:id/status
- POST /api/mobile/jobs/:id/complete
- POST /api/mobile/jobs/:id/photos
- POST /api/mobile/cod/collect
- GET /api/mobile/notifications

Rules:

- Mobile app must not store external API secrets.
- Mobile calls backend only.
- Technician can only see own jobs.
- Driver can only see own deliveries.
- COD collection creates payment + audit log.

Acceptance:

- API ready for Expo/React Native or Android app.
- Permissions enforced.

# ==================================================

FINAL QA

After completing the phases, run full QA.

Check:

- TypeScript strict
- Build passes
- No frontend secrets
- Auth required
- RBAC works
- DB migrations work
- Seed works
- Order rules work
- Payment rules work
- Webhook idempotency works
- Audit logs work
- Integration logs work
- Pagination works
- UI Arabic RTL works
- Tests pass
- Docker works

Final output:

1. Summary of completed phases
2. Files created/changed
3. Database schema summary
4. API summary
5. New environment variables
6. Remaining risks
7. How to run locally
8. How to deploy
9. Next recommended development milestone
issues.title: انشئ مشروع جديد وقم بكل المطلوب بالكامل الان

issues.description:
## Input
From VENDOR BRIEF: Bolt.new — benchmark sweep May 2026 ([DEV-76](mention://issue/ee26fb58-8f56-41fc-9543-ca2f5c230f85)), the model-selection-per-task pattern is marked **Adopt**:

> "expose model choice at issue-creation time; fast/cheap model for boilerplate, Opus Max-tier per issue type"

Validation signal: bolt.diy (19.4k stars, exceeds Bolt.new's 16k) — provider/model flexibility is the top community-requested feature. devosnt's squad model already has natural task boundaries; this adds the routing layer on top.

## Expected Output
1. Issue-creation CLI/workflow updated to accept a `model_tier` field (`standard` | `max`).
2. Agent assignment logic (or issue template guidance) routes by tier: boilerplate tasks (scaffolding, CRUD generation, style passes) → Standard; complex reasoning tasks (architecture, security review, QA gate, multi-system integrations) → Max (Opus Max-tier).
3. Sprint Commander skill updated to set `model_tier` when creating sub-issues.
4. Documentation (CLAUDE.md or agent operating manuals) updated to reflect the new field.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `model_tier` can be specified at issue creation time.
- [ ] Default tier rules documented per task type (boilerplate → standard, complex → max).
- [ ] Sprint Commander creates sub-issues with correct tier pre-set.
- [ ] At least one example sprint shows the split in action (one standard sub-issue, one max sub-issue).
- [ ] No regression in existing issue-creation flow.

## Dependencies
- [DEV-76](mention://issue/ee26fb58-8f56-41fc-9543-ca2f5c230f85) — source brief (must be in_review or done first).

## Risk
- If `multica issue create` does not support a `model_tier` metadata key today, the initial implementation is a convention (metadata key) rather than a hard-typed field — acceptable as v1 until platform support lands.
- Over-classifying tasks as Max increases cost without quality benefit; default rules must be conservative.

## Definition of Done
- Acceptance criteria above all checked.
- Sprint Commander operating manual updated.
- Security review not required (no user-facing surface, no auth changes).
issues.title: Implement model-selection-per-task routing (Standard vs Max tier)

issues.description:
Run the daily factory health audit. Cover: (1) runtime health (multica runtime list) — alert if any offline >5m; (2) WIP discipline — flag any agent with >3 in_progress or any squad with >6; (3) stale issues — list any in_progress issue >24h with no comments; (4) orphan/zero-skill check — every skill assigned to ≥1 agent; every agent has ≥1 skill; (5) metadata hygiene — sample 10 done issues and flag any with stale waiting_on / blocked_reason; (6) cost — pull the current month spend per project and compare to budget (cost-budget-tracker); (7) error budget — pull yesterday's Sentry error rate, latency p95, uptime % per shipped app and alert on SLO burn. Post one consolidated comment on the created issue with: GREEN/YELLOW/RED status, the top 5 issues found, and one-line remediation per issue. Status the issue 'done' if all green; 'in_review' if YELLOW; 'blocked' if RED. Do not silently ignore failures.

---
*Autopilot run triggered at 2026-05-27 03:00 UTC. After starting work, rename this issue to accurately reflect what you are doing.*
issues.title: Factory Health Audit — 2026-05-27

issues.description:
## Input
`dashboard-screen-planner` covers dashboards. There is no equivalent for general screens (auth, settings, onboarding, marketing, modal flows). Every UX-architect run rebuilds the structure by hand.

## Expected Output
Skill `ux-screen-generator`. Input: screen purpose (one sentence). Output: a structured spec:
- Primary CTA + secondary actions
- Data shown (fields + format)
- Permission gate (which role(s) can reach this)
- Empty / loading / error state variants (mandatory)
- Mobile breakpoint (≤ 375px) layout note
- RTL variant note (when target locale includes Arabic)
- Accessibility minimums (focus order, ARIA labels, contrast)
- Recommended components from the workspace design tokens

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Output is implementation-ready (a frontend-engineer can build directly from it)
- [ ] Reused by `prd-to-user-stories` + `dashboard-screen-planner`
- [ ] Includes the 3 mandatory variants every time

## DoD
Skill published; product-manager + ux-architect agents have it in their skill list.
issues.title: SKILL: ux-screen-generator — general screen spec beyond dashboards (auth/settings/onboarding)
